evaluation.summary: Empirical scaling of the variance-to-mean ratio for primorial gaps yields an exponent of about 1.17 for k≥3, which does not match the hypothesized 0.56 exponent.
next_directions: Extend the computation of R(k) to larger primorials (k>8) using high-precision arithmetic to assess asymptotic behavior.
Develop or locate a theoretical model that predicts the variance-to-mean ratio scaling and test its predictions against the extended data.
Investigate the origin of the discrepancy between the observed exponent (~1.17) and the conjectured 0.56, including possible boundary or finite-size effects.
